reasoning: User asked specifically for fireworks festivals (花火大会). The tool returned 312 general festivals — none of the visible items are fireworks events; all are shrine festivals (重要無形民俗文化財). The core constraint — 花火大会 — was completely ignored. Data is real and grounded but entirely misaligned with intent.
missing_data: Actual fireworks festivals (花火大会) such as Sumida River Fireworks, Nagaoka, PL Fireworks. No keyword filter for 花火 was applied.
improvement_hint: Add keyword/category filter to get_festivals so it can match 花火大会 specifically, or add a fireworks boolean flag in the festival schema.

reasoning: Same pattern as L3-01 but in Japanese. Query is about 花火, response returns 312 general intangible folk cultural heritage festivals with no fireworks relevance. Language field correctly set to ja, but content filter for 花火大会 not applied. Essentially identical failure to L3-01.
missing_data: 花火大会 specific festivals: 隅田川花火大会、長岡花火、PL花火など。花火カテゴリのフィルターが必要。
improvement_hint: get_festivals should support q= or keyword filter. Alternatively tag festivals with event_type=fireworks in the source data.

reasoning: Query: snow festivals in Japan. Tool filtered to Hokkaido (correct region) but returned 0 local items — falls back to national_heritage which are UNESCO festival entries (Kawagoe Hikawa Festival, Tatemon, etc.) none of which are snow festivals. Sapporo Snow Festival not present. Total constraint failure on topic.
missing_data: さっぽろ雪まつり (Sapporo Snow Festival), Towada Kanko Festival, Hirosaki Castle Snow Lantern Festival — the canonical snow festival answers are missing entirely.
wrong_data: national_heritage fallback returns non-Hokkaido, non-snow festivals like Kawagoe Hikawa (Saitama).
improvement_hint: Index snow festivals (雪まつり) explicitly with keyword tags. When prefecture filter returns 0, do not fall back to unrelated national heritage — return empty with a suggestion to broaden scope.

reasoning: User asked for rural life experience (田舎暮らし体験) in Chinese. Tool returned Japan Heritage stories starting with Asuka/Nara historical narratives (women in ancient Japan, Kamakura). These are cultural history stories, not rural living experiences. Chinese language output is correct (zh confirmed). Content-intent mismatch is fundamental.
missing_data: Rural homestay programs, satoyama experiences, agritourism, village cultural landscapes like Shirakawa-go or Nishi-Awa (Japan Heritage 054 covers it). Should filter on rural/agricultural themes.
improvement_hint: Add theme_filter support for 農村/里山/田舎 in get_japan_heritage. Story 011 (Asuka) and 025 (Kamakura) are not relevant to rural life and should not rank first.

reasoning: Korean query for autumn foliage spots. Query term 紅葉 returns 81 results with real coordinates and heritage designations. Top results include Momijidani Park (Hiroshima, prominence 0.75 with Important Cultural Property), Momijidani (Kyoto), Sakurajima, etc. heritage_designations_labels present. Some results (stations, paintings) are not foliage viewing spots — ranking could be better, but core data is usable.
missing_data: Famous koyo spots like Nikko, Arashiyama, Korankei are not surfaced in truncated output. No season/date context for peak foliage timing.
improvement_hint: Add prominence-based re-ranking for search_area; filter out non-visitables (paintings, station QIDs) from koyo queries. Add seasonal metadata if possible.

reasoning: User wants a quiet, non-touristy meditation temple. Query 修行 returns mixed results: Shugyō-ji (Tokyo — urban, not quiet), STAY YAMAGATA scrape noise (completely irrelevant), Manjushuin (Kyoto Important Cultural Property — somewhat relevant but touristy). Municipal scrape noise (Chinese/Traditional Chinese tourism portal content) pollutes results. The intent for quiet/non-touristy is not captured.
missing_data: Koya-san shukubo stays, Eiheiji monastery (Fukui), Tofuku-ji Zen retreat — canonical meditation/ascetic practice venues absent.
wrong_data: STAY YAMAGATA entries are completely off-topic noise from municipal scrapes matching 修行 tangentially.
improvement_hint: Filter municipal_scrape entries by relevance threshold for search_area. Add monastic_stay or shukubo type to attraction schema. Boost religious_building + Buddhist_temple results with 修行 context.

reasoning: User asked to see northern lights (auroras) in Japan — this is an impossible/near-impossible ask (aurora sightings in Japan are extremely rare, only in extreme northern Hokkaido during strong solar storms). The search for オーロラ returns 84 results but all are noise: cookie consent pages, Sanrio Puroland, Furano DMO tourism plans. Zero aurora-specific content. The tool fails to flag this as out-of-scope or near-impossible.
missing_data: A factual response that auroras are rarely visible in Japan; Wakkanai/northern Hokkaido as the only possibility during extreme geomagnetic storms. No such content exists in the corpus.
wrong_data: Sanrio Puroland returned as an オーロラ result — likely via show/event name matching not actual aurora content.
improvement_hint: Add out-of-scope detection: if query matches phenomena not reliably present in Japan (aurora, safari animals), return a factual note. Improve scrape noise filtering — cookie consent pages should be excluded.

reasoning: User asks specifically for 100-year-old traditional houses (古民家) to sleep in. Tool returned get_hotels(Gifu) which is geographically plausible (Gifu has Shirakawa-go), but results show generic hotels: Airbnb apartment, Route Inn, hostel, standard hotels. No ancient farmhouse (古民家) lodging specifically identified. The 古民家 constraint is unaddressed.
missing_data: Shirakawa-go gassho-zukuri farmhouse stays, Hida Folk Village area accommodations, machiya stays in Takayama old town — specifically tagged as historic buildings.
improvement_hint: Add lodging_type=kominka or historic_building flag to hotel schema. Allow search_area with q=古民家 to surface lodging options. get_hotels lacks heritage/age metadata.

reasoning: Thai query for off-the-beaten-path cherry blossom spots. Query 桜 returns 164 results. Top hits include Sakurada-mon gate (prominence 0.9, Important Cultural Property), Sakuragi Station, Sakurai-jinja, Sakurajima — these are places named 桜 but not cherry blossom viewing spots. Intent is 穴場 (hidden gems) for hanami; the response cannot distinguish 桜 as character in name vs. cherry blossom venue.
missing_data: Actual cherry blossom hidden gems: Hinokinaigawa, Takato Castle, Hinata Yoshino — less-known hanami spots. No seasonal/hanami filter available.
improvement_hint: Distinguish name-match vs. semantic-match for 桜. Tag attractions with event_type=hanami or seasonal_highlight=cherry_blossom. Filter by prominence < 0.8 for 穴場 queries.

reasoning: Photography/dramatic landscape query. 絶景 search returns 51 results. Strong hits include Onigajo (Important Scenic Beauty + Natural Monument, Mie), Kisakata (Nationally-designated Natural Monument, Akita), Niigata zekkei feature spot with specific description. heritage_designations_labels present. Top results are municipal scrape noise (cookie pages, Yamagata portal in Chinese). Ordering could prioritize Wikidata heritage attractions over scrape noise.
missing_data: Mt. Fuji viewpoints, Tottori Sand Dunes, Aogashima — iconic dramatic landscape spots not in truncated results. No photography-specific metadata.
improvement_hint: Rank Wikidata heritage attractions above municipal scrape noise for 絶景 queries. Add landscape photography category or natural scenery tag to attractions.

reasoning: Arabic query for natural outdoor hot springs (not hotel baths). 露天風呂 search returns 72 results with relevant content: Iwate onsen descriptions with outdoor baths, Yamaguchi day-trip outdoor onsen feature, Hosenbou Onsen (Ehime, with specific outdoor bath + scenic description), Haraigawa Onsen. Several entries are genuinely useful. Main issue: results are in Japanese (not Arabic), and some are generic portal noise.
missing_data: Specific renowned open-air/riverside onsen like Kawayu Onsen (Hokkaido river bathing), Okama onsen — canonical nature onsen destinations. Arabic language output not served.
improvement_hint: Ensure municipal_scrape content is translated or at least summarized in requested lang=ar. Add outdoor=true or rotenburo=true flag to filter hotel-baths vs. nature baths.

reasoning: User wants to swim with wild dolphins. イルカ search returns 61 results but top visible items are: Kyoto cultural ambassador page (completely irrelevant), Noto Island Aquarium (captures dolphin show — relevant but captive, not wild), Teradomari fish market (irrelevant). The wild dolphin swimming distinction (Mikurajima, Amami) is not surfaced. Reasoning failure: data exists (Amami tourism mentions dolphins) but it's buried.
missing_data: Mikurajima wild dolphin swimming (Tokyo islands), Amami Oshima dolphin encounters — the primary Japan locations for wild dolphin swimming.
wrong_data: Kyoto cultural ambassador page has no dolphin relevance — irrelevant scrape result dominating top position.
improvement_hint: Improve relevance filtering for municipal_scrapes: exclude pages where query term appears only incidentally. Add wildlife encounter type tag to attractions.

reasoning: User asks for traditional architecture untouched by modernization — ideal answer: 重伝建 (Important Preservation Districts). Tool returned get_japan_heritage() with no filter, again starting with Asuka/Kamakura narratives. While these are cultural heritage stories, they don't specifically point to intact streetscapes. The 重伝建 districts (Shirakawa-go, Tsumago, Gion, etc.) are not surfaced from Japan Heritage alone.
missing_data: 重要伝統的建造物群保存地区 (Preservation Districts) — Shirakawa-go, Tsumago-juku, Higashiyama Kyoto. A dedicated tool or filter for architectural preservation districts is needed.
improvement_hint: Add a get_preservation_districts tool or tag Japan Heritage stories with architecture/streetscape theme so they rank higher for traditional architecture queries. Story 011 (Asuka) is not about traditional buildings.

reasoning: Vietnamese query for Ainu culture experience in Hokkaido. Excellent result: top attractions are directly on-point — Sarugawa River Ainu Cultural Landscape (Important Cultural Landscape), Ainu Museum (former, coordinates), National Ainu Museum (Upopoy, Shiraoi), Nibutani Ainu Culture Museum (Biratori), Hokkaido Ainu Center (Sapporo), Kawamura Kaneto Museum (Asahikawa), Teshikaga Kotan Museum. All in Hokkaido. High prominence scores. heritage_designations_labels present.
missing_data: Upopoy (Symbolic Space for Ethnic Harmony) is listed as National Ainu Museum which is correct. Could add experiential programs (craft workshops, dance). Minor: no Vietnamese-language descriptions.
improvement_hint: Add experience_type=cultural_workshop tag to attractions offering hands-on Ainu programs. Consider translating attraction descriptions to Vietnamese for lang=vi requests.

reasoning: Query for cliff-carved Buddhist statues (磨崖仏). Excellent precision: top 2 results are Usuki Stone Buddhas (Oita, National Treasure + Special Historic Site, prominence 0.9) and Oya-dera (Tochigi, Special Historic Site, prominence 0.9) — the two most canonical magaibutsu sites. Kiyomizu Magaibutsu (Kagoshima), Oiwa Koboin magaibutsu (Fukuoka) also present. heritage_designations_labels richly populated. Minor noise: Rankeido Daruma painting (Yamanashi) slips in, not a magaibutsu.
missing_data: Takachiho Gorge area magaibutsu, Kunisaki Peninsula cluster (Oita) could be more prominent. Minor gap.
wrong_data: 蘭渓道隆賛達磨図 is a painting (tangible cultural property), not a cliff carving — minor noise in results.
improvement_hint: Filter out 2D artwork QIDs (paintings, scrolls) from magaibutsu search. Consider adding a kinds=rock_carving or sculptural_site type.

reasoning: User wants day-trip volcano hikes from Tokyo. Key constraints: day-trip distance from Tokyo, hikeable volcano. Search 火山 returns results dominated by Aso volcano (Kumamoto — 1000km from Tokyo, not a day trip) via Chinese-language municipal scrapes. No Mt. Fuji, Hakone, or Nikko volcanic areas in visible results. The from-Tokyo constraint is completely ignored; Aso content is misleading for a Tokyo-based traveler.
missing_data: Mt. Fuji (Shizuoka/Yamanashi, day-trip accessible), Hakone caldera, Tanigawa-dake, Oshima Island (Tokyo) volcanic options — canonical Tokyo-accessible volcano hike answers.
wrong_data: Aso volcano content dominates but is 1000+ km from Tokyo — misleads on travel_feasibility for day trip constraint.
improvement_hint: Add geographic proximity filtering to search_area when query includes location context. Rank by distance from specified base (Tokyo). Add travel_time_from_tokyo metadata to major attractions.

reasoning: Indonesian query for freshly-made tofu at a temple. Query 豆腐 returns 93 results but top items include: Fukui portal (irrelevant), Iwate ryokan (no tofu), Kumamoto food guide (no temple). Best result is GI product Iwaideyama Kori-tofu (freeze-dried tofu from Miyagi) — a product designation, not a temple experience. Zen temple tofu cuisine (精進料理, 湯豆腐) context not surfaced.
missing_data: Nanzen-ji yudofu restaurants (Kyoto), Eiheiji shojin ryori (Fukui), Koya-san temple cuisine — the canonical temple-tofu experiences.
improvement_hint: Add cuisine/food_experience type to search_area results. Index shojin ryori and temple cuisine entries specifically. Municipal scrape noise about Kumamoto and Iwate dominates for 豆腐 without temple context.

reasoning: User wants time-frozen fishing villages. 漁港 search returns 84 results. Useful content: Teradomari fish market (Niigata, with specific shop list), Kesennuma harbor town DMO page (evocative description of fishing port culture and Sanriku coast), Noyako Bay (Hokkaido, Ramsar Wetland). Fishing village character partially captured. Main limitation: actual preserved villages (Ouchi-juku, Wajima, Sanriku fishing hamlets) not specifically surfaced.
missing_data: Wajima (Noto, traditional fishing + lacquerware), Tomonoura (Hiroshima, historically preserved port), Dejima area — canonical 'frozen in time' fishing village answers.
improvement_hint: Tag preservation_status=historic or traditional_district to fishing ports that qualify as preserved streetscapes. Kitakyushu 海を楽しむ page adds no value — improve scrape quality threshold.

reasoning: User wants to learn traditional washi papermaking. get_local_specialty(craft) returns 231 items. Results are METI Traditional Crafts (densan) — authentic official data. Items shown are textiles (Nibutani Attus Ainu weaving, Oitama Tsumugi silk, Ugo Shina Cloth), not washi paper. Washi likely exists in the 231 items but isn't ranked first. The craft category is correct but washi-specific filtering not applied.
missing_data: Echizen Washi (Fukui), Mino Washi (Gifu), Ogawa Washi (Saitama) — the three major washi traditions. These exist in METI densan database but are not visible in truncated results due to no keyword filter.
improvement_hint: Add q= keyword search to get_local_specialty to filter within category. Washi should rank first for 和紙 query within craft. The tool needs combined category + keyword filtering.

reasoning: Japanese query for period drama scenery (jidaigeki). 宿場 (post town) search returns 90 results. Top spots are excellent: Kiso-ji Suharajuku/Nojiriku article (Nagano, Edo-period post towns with detailed description), Yanagimachi Ueda castle town (300-year-old machiya), Kyunatemachijuku (Important Cultural Property + Historic Site, Wakayama), Shukunegi (Important Preservation District, Sado Island, Niigata). heritage_designations_labels present. Akasaka Detached Palace (Tokyo) is irrelevant noise.
missing_data: Tsumago-juku and Magome-juku (Nagano, the most iconic jidaigeki-style post towns) — probably in results beyond truncation. Minor omission.
wrong_data: Akasaka Detached Palace (赤坂離宮) is a Western-style state guesthouse — irrelevant for jidaigeki scenery.
improvement_hint: Filter out Western-style architectural QIDs for 宿場 queries. Tsumago/Magome should appear near top — check ranking logic.

reasoning: User wants uncrowded beaches. ビーチ returns 81 results. Beaches found: Naminoue (Okinawa, prominence 0.75), Hamasaka Kenmin (Hyogo, prominence 0.75), Sunayama Beach (Okinawa, 0.75), Tropical Beach (Okinawa, 0.65), Painagama Beach (Okinawa), Yonehara Beach (Ishigaki). Okinawa-heavy. The 穴場/not crowded constraint cannot be served from this data — no crowd-level metadata exists. Theme park entries (Minami Chita Beach Land, Oiso Long Beach) are aquaparks, not natural beaches.
missing_data: Crowd-level or popularity metadata to distinguish busy vs. quiet beaches. Remote beaches like Ago Bay, Kitayama-hama (Miyazaki), Shimoda are not surfaced.
wrong_data: 南知多ビーチランド and 大磯ロングビーチ are water amusement parks, not natural beaches — misleading for the query.
improvement_hint: Add is_amusement_park flag to filter beach parks vs. natural beaches. Add crowd_level or popularity_index metadata. Distinguish natural beach from developed resort.

reasoning: User wants old-school izakaya drinking alleys (横丁). Search returns 79 results. Visible items: a Shinagawa park (completely irrelevant), Saga tourism portal (no izakaya content), Haikara-tto Yokocho (Yamaguchi amusement park), Doro-hakkei (scenic beauty, Mie), National Treasure cultural artifacts from Kyoto. The actual izakaya yokocho (Yurakucho, Shinjuku, Osaka Dotonbori) are not present. Reasoning failure: data about actual drinking alleys not in corpus or poorly ranked.
missing_data: Omoide Yokocho (Shinjuku), Yurakucho alley, Hozenji Yokocho (Osaka), Nishiki Market area — canonical old-school izakaya destinations.
wrong_data: Cultural artifact QIDs (Yokobe silk scarf — national treasure) have no izakaya relevance; 横 in name triggered false match.
improvement_hint: Add food/nightlife venue type to search_area. Filter out artifact/object QIDs from place-based queries. Tag izakaya districts explicitly in municipal scrape data.

reasoning: German query for rural rail travel in Japan. ローカル線 search returns 58 results with genuinely useful content: Nagano local railway art trip feature (Ueda Dentetsu Bessho Line — specific route, stops, frequency details), Ibaraki coastal rail + Hitachi Seaside Park model course, DMO Tsugaru plan mentioning 2 local railways, Kyushu DMO railway content. Response is in Japanese (not German) but content is highly relevant and actionable.
missing_data: Specific train names beyond Ueda Dentetsu: Akechi Railway, Kurobe Gorge Railway, Sagano Scenic Railway — more variety would help. German translation not provided.
improvement_hint: Translate or summarize municipal scrape content into requested language (de). Consider adding a rail_type=local tag to attractions near scenic local railways.

reasoning: Summer firefly viewing query. ホタル returns 72 results with strong relevant content: Hotaru-no-Sato park (Hyogo, coordinates), Toyota Firefly Museum (Yamaguchi, coordinates), Kitakyushu 蛍観賞 page, Toyoda-cho firefly boat tour (Yamaguchi, detailed — date, price ¥2000/1000, 27-year history, 800m river, booking info), Nagasaki firefly guide (timing: 8-9pm, etiquette notes), Ishikawa tourism site. Excellent practical detail in Toyoda-cho entry. Minor noise: Iwate generic feature page.
missing_data: Tatsuno City (Hyogo) firefly famous spot, Shiitake-dani (Nara) — additional spots would broaden coverage. Specific viewing dates for most entries absent.
improvement_hint: Add seasonal_period metadata (e.g. June) to firefly entries. The Toyoda-cho entry is exemplary — ensure similar detail for other spots. Iwate generic scrape noise should be filtered.

reasoning: Russian query for crane (ツル/журавли) wintering spots in Japan. 鶴 search returns 161 results. Top items: Tsurugaoka Hachimangu (Kamakura shrine — named 鶴 but no cranes), Tsurukawa Kakurinji Temple halls (Hyogo), Kakurin-ji temple — all religious sites named with 鶴 character but not crane viewing locations. Arasaki (Kagoshima) — the world's largest wintering crane site — not visible in truncated results. Total failure on the wildlife constraint.
missing_data: Arasaki Crane Observation Center (Izumi, Kagoshima) — world's #1 wintering crane site with up to 16,000 cranes. Kushiro wetlands (Hokkaido, Japanese Red-crowned Cranes year-round). These are the canonical answers.
improvement_hint: Add wildlife_observation=true and species tags to nature reserves and wetlands. Tsurugaoka Hachimangu should not rank for crane-wildlife queries — add disambiguation for place-name vs. animal queries.
